expression: a 2-pool root (mobile water+protein pool feeding the xylem + a slow-exchanging lipid/cell-wall bound store holding the measured root burden) reproduces the long-chain shoot WHILE keeping the measured root high, closing the LC3 single-pool tradeoff => support; it cannot match root and shoot simultaneously => refute
finding:
validation/twopool_longchain.py (ORYZA2000): the 2-pool root (mobile + slow lipid/cell-wall bound store; lipid loads from the mobile pool) matches MID-LONG chains root AND shoot SIMULTANEOUSLY -- PFDA(C10) root 3.5 vs 4.2, straw 5.0 vs 3.5, grain 4.1 vs 3.4; PFUnDA(C11) root 9.8 vs 19.5 (~2x), straw 13.4 vs 8.2, grain 3.6 vs 3.1. The single pool could not (LC3 root drain). Shoot RMSE 0.32.
validation/twopool_longchain.py: PFDoDA(C12) FAILS -- root collapses to 1.2 vs 69.3 (mobile pool rm=0.02 starves -> bound store rb only 1.2; straw 8.6 vs 49.7, grain 26 vs 45.5). The mobile-pool soil uptake (jR) is too small to sustain a high bound root AND feed the shoot: a mass-balance/UPTAKE limit, not internal distribution. The 2-pool does not close the longest chain.
